AMD.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 after market close = 3 trading days away. This is a high-magnitude binary catalyst well within the 1-10 day window. Consensus EPS $1.18, revenue ~$9.8B (+32% YoY). Tariff headwind (25% on advanced AI chips) and Nvidia’s 89% server GPU share retention are ongoing negatives. Overall sentiment: mixed/priced-in. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Net selling — CEO Lisa Su sold ~$16M in March 2026; board-level ratio is 83 buys vs 188 sells (2:1 sellers). No fresh insider trades in immediate window but the distribution overhang is a bearish lean. TECHNICAL SETUP — POOR/EXTENDED: AMD has run +74.3% in 21 trading days. Daily RSI-14 = 83.06, RSI-21 = 86.46 (deeply overbought). Z-score vs 50-period = 2.6. Price is 52.6% above 50-SMA and 68.5% above 200-SMA. Bollinger %B = 0.929 (near upper band). Hourly ADX = 17.25 (trend weakening intraday). This is a fully exhausted chart entering a binary event. Analyst consensus targets ($274–$290 avg, median $290) are well below current price ($354); even the bull targets (Evercore $358, Melius $380) are near or barely above current price. The risk/reward going long here is extremely unfavorable — the street has already baked in the bull case. CATALYST RISK: Classic 'buy the rumor, sell the news' setup. Price has discounted a strong Q1 beat and raised guidance scenario. Any miss, in-line print, or guidance reset will accelerate mean reversion toward the $310–$328 support cluster. Pre-earnings entry on the long side violates the constructive-setup requirement.
